contract:
  schema_version: 1
  scope:
    question: What exact correlator, thermal, and operator-dictionary requirements must be satisfied before this project can call DSSYK a full semiclassical de Sitter bulk dual?
    in_scope:
      - Translate the project contract into a phase-level decision rubric.
      - State what evidence is sufficient for a constrained-sector correspondence but insufficient for a full bulk dual.
  context_intake:
    must_read_refs:
      - Ref-2209-09997
      - Ref-2310-16994
      - Ref-2402-02584
      - Ref-2404-03535
    must_include_prior_outputs:
      - GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/01-01-SUMMARY.md
  claims:
    - id: claim-decision-rubric
      statement: The phase defines a decision rubric that distinguishes correlator-only success, constrained-sector bulk correspondence, and a full semiclassical dS bulk dual.
      deliverables:
        - deliv-decision-criteria
        - deliv-comparison-rubric
      acceptance_tests:
        - test-full-dual-threshold
        - test-proxy-rejection
      references:
        - Ref-2209-09997
        - Ref-2310-16994
        - Ref-2402-02584
        - Ref-2404-03535
  deliverables:
    - id: deliv-decision-criteria
      kind: derivation
      path: GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/decision-criteria.md
      description: Phase-level statement of the minimum criteria for a full semiclassical dS bulk dual.
    - id: deliv-comparison-rubric
      kind: derivation
      path: GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/comparison-rubric.md
      description: Rubric for comparing the anchor papers against correlator, thermal, and dictionary tests.
  references:
    - id: Ref-2209-09997
      kind: paper
      locator: Adel A. Rahman, dS JT Gravity and Double-Scaled SYK, arXiv:2209.09997
      role: must_consider
      why_it_matters: Provides the positive dS JT proposal but leaves the stronger full-dual standard open.
      applies_to:
        - claim-decision-rubric
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
    - id: Ref-2310-16994
      kind: paper
      locator: Vladimir Narovlansky and Herman Verlinde, Double-scaled SYK and de Sitter Holography, arXiv:2310.16994
      role: benchmark
      why_it_matters: Central correlator benchmark whose doubled-sector status must be classified correctly.
      applies_to:
        - claim-decision-rubric
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
    - id: Ref-2402-02584
      kind: paper
      locator: Herman Verlinde and Mengyang Zhang, SYK Correlators from 2D Liouville-de Sitter Gravity, arXiv:2402.02584
      role: benchmark
      why_it_matters: Sharpest exact correlator evidence, but not automatically a full thermal or operator-dictionary argument.
      applies_to:
        - claim-decision-rubric
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
    - id: Ref-2404-03535
      kind: paper
      locator: Andreas Blommaert, Thomas G. Mertens, and Jacopo Papalini, The dilaton gravity hologram of double-scaled SYK, arXiv:2404.03535
      role: must_consider
      why_it_matters: Supplies the fake-temperature and auxiliary-system caveats that can block a full-dual upgrade.
      applies_to:
        - claim-decision-rubric
      must_surface: true
      required_actions:
        - read
        - compare
        - cite
  acceptance_tests:
    - id: test-full-dual-threshold
      subject: claim-decision-rubric
      kind: consistency
      procedure: Confirm that the rubric requires correlator evidence together with a coherent thermal and operator-dictionary interpretation before a full-dual verdict is allowed.
      pass_condition: The criteria document names at least one non-correlator requirement that must be satisfied for a full-dual classification.
      evidence_required:
        - deliv-decision-criteria
        - deliv-comparison-rubric
    - id: test-proxy-rejection
      subject: claim-decision-rubric
      kind: human_review
      procedure: Confirm that correlator-only success in a doubled or auxiliary construction is classified below full duality unless the rubric states why that restriction is decisive rather than auxiliary.
      pass_condition: The rubric explicitly rejects correlator-only or proxy-only success as sufficient for the full claim.
      evidence_required:
        - deliv-decision-criteria
        - Ref-2310-16994
        - Ref-2402-02584
        - Ref-2404-03535
  forbidden_proxies:
    - id: fp-correlator-only
      subject: claim-decision-rubric
      proxy: Treating an exact or semiclassical two-point-function match as sufficient evidence for a full semiclassical dS bulk dual.
      reason: The project contract requires thermal and operator-dictionary consistency in addition to correlator evidence.
  uncertainty_markers:
    weakest_anchors:
      - Whether fake temperature can be promoted to an acceptable semiclassical bulk temperature.
    disconfirming_observations:
      - The best positive evidence remains confined to a doubled or auxiliary sector after the rubric is applied.
---

<objective>Define the exact threshold between a constrained-sector DSSYK correspondence and a full semiclassical de Sitter bulk dual.</objective>

<execution_context>Use the anchor extraction from Plan 01 together with the approved project contract to write the decision rules that govern the rest of the project.</execution_context>

<tasks>
  <task type="auto">
    <name>Task 1: Write the decision criteria</name>
    <files>GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/decision-criteria.md</files>
    <action>Translate the project contract into explicit requirements for full duality, constrained-sector correspondence, and failure of the simple full-dual claim.</action>
    <verify>Ensure the criteria include at least one correlator requirement and at least one thermal or dictionary requirement.</verify>
    <done>The project can say exactly why a correlator-only match does or does not settle the user's question.</done>
  </task>
  <task type="auto">
    <name>Task 2: Build the comparison rubric</name>
    <files>GPD/phases/01-anchor-audit-and-decision-criteria/comparison-rubric.md</files>
    <action>Create a reusable rubric that scores each anchor paper against correlator, thermal, and operator-dictionary tests plus explicit caveats.</action>
    <verify>Check that the rubric can classify Rahman, Narovlansky-Verlinde, Verlinde-Zhang, and Blommaert-Mertens-Papalini on the same scale.</verify>
    <done>The next phases can compare candidate bulk models without changing the decision standard midstream.</done>
  </task>
</tasks>

<verification>Fail closed if the rubric would still allow a full-dual verdict from a doubled-sector or exact-correlator result alone without a coherent thermal and operator-dictionary story.</verification>

<success_criteria>The project has an explicit, reusable decision rubric that blocks false progress and distinguishes full duality from weaker correspondence claims.</success_criteria>
